subject: [PATCH v2 2/5] mm/huge_memory: refuse to split a file folio when the anchor is beyond EOF

2026-07-14.patch_summary: This patch prevents the Linux kernel from splitting a file folio when its anchor is beyond the end of the file (EOF). The split operation relies on the caller's locked folio to keep the inode alive, but if the anchor lies beyond EOF, it can cause the inode to be evicted and freed while still being used by the split operation. To prevent this, the patch checks if the anchor is at or beyond EOF and returns an error (-EBUSY) in such cases. This fix ensures that the split operation only proceeds when the anchor is within the valid range of the file.
